content: Compound interest is a powerful way to grow your savings over time. Unlike simple interest, which earns returns only on your initial amount, compound interest earns returns on both your original investment and the interest that accumulates. Think of it as “interest on interest.” This means your money doesn’t just grow linearly; it grows exponentially. The longer you leave your savings untouched, the more dramatic the growth becomes. For example, investing $10,000 at a 5% annual compound interest rate will grow to about $16,000 in 10 years, but over 20 years, it nearly doubles again to around $26,500. This effect can significantly increase your wealth, especially when you start early and allow your investment to mature. Compound interest rewards patience and consistency, making it an essential concept for building long-term financial security with minimal effort.
